subtasks.description: Query vendor reliability data to calculate risk scores including incident history, uptime metrics, and other reliability indicators
Classify each vendor into risk categories (low_risk, medium_risk, high_risk) based on incident history and calculated risk scores from the reliability query

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks needed: (1) sql_gen to query vendor reliability metrics and incident data from SQL warehouse—this requires joins across vendor, incident, and uptime tables, making it 'mid' tier; (2) cross_recon to classify vendors by risk tier based on the queried results—classification logic against incident history thresholds is part of the reconciliation/decision layer, assigned 'mid' tier since it applies multi-signal logic (incidents, scores, thresholds). This avoids unnecessary splits; both queries could be combined into one SQL+classification, but separating data retrieval from policy-driven classification makes the pipeline more maintainable. Not a policy_action because this is vendor risk classification, not governance/compliance approval."

output.sql: SELECT vendor_name, total_invoices, paid_invoices, overdue_invoices, avg_days_to_pay, total_amount_usd, paid_amount_usd, ROUND(paid_invoices::NUMERIC / NULLIF(total_invoices, 0) * 100, 2) AS payment_reliability_pct, ROUND(overdue_invoices::NUMERIC / NULLIF(total_invoices, 0) * 100, 2) AS overdue_rate_pct, CASE WHEN ROUND(overdue_invoices::NUMERIC / NULLIF(total_invoices, 0) * 100, 2) <= 10 AND avg_days_to_pay <= 30 THEN 'low_risk' WHEN ROUND(overdue_invoices::NUMERIC / NULLIF(total_invoices, 0) * 100, 2) <= 30 AND avg_days_to_pay <= 60 THEN 'medium_risk' ELSE 'high_risk' END AS risk_classification FROM (SELECT vendor_name, COUNT(*) AS total_invoices, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS paid_invoices, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'overdue' OR (due_date < CURRENT_DATE AND (paid_date IS NULL OR status != 'paid')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_invoices, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN paid_date IS NOT NULL THEN paid_date - invoice_date ELSE NULL END), 2) AS avg_days_to_pay, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS paid_amount_usd FROM invoice_facts GROUP BY vendor_name) vendor_stats ORDER BY overdue_rate_pct DESC, avg_days_to_pay DESC
